mnemonics: All Normals Obey Variance And Nothing Overtakes
A Nice Oven Always Yields Beautiful And Nice
keyPoints: Basic Principle of ANOVA: Compare means of different groups to see if at least one group mean is different.
One-way ANOVA: Used to compare means of two or more groups.
Two-way ANOVA: Used to compare means of two or more factors with interaction between them.
Latin-square Design: A type of experimental design used to reduce confounding variables.
Analysis of Co-Variance: Used to compare means of two or more groups when variance is unequal.
